title: Mechanical engineers (professional)
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture. May recommend improvements in testing equipment and techniques. Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products. Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. (Based on 35 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Report experimental results by writing papers for scientific journals or presenting information at scientific conferences.
Collaborate with other scientists or engineers in the design, development, and testing of equipment, instrumentation, and procedures.
Analyze research data to determine significance and modify or adjust equipment to meet specifications.
Supervise or train project team members, technicians, and others to ensure efficient project execution.
Test material samples to determine conformance to specifications of mechanical properties and performance.
Design, implement, and monitor the development of systems or equipment related to mechanical or fluid energy.
Prepare technical reports, proposals, and specifications for engineering and management personnel.
Conduct field tests and monitor test equipment to comply with requirements for evaluation and analysis.
Design specialized or customized mechanical systems, machines, or components for various applications.
Develop, modify, or research methods of processing and testing materials for enhanced performance or new applications.
Test aircraft systems under simulated operational conditions, performing systems readiness tests and pre- and post-operational checkouts to establish design or fabrication parameters.
Fabricate and install parts and systems to be tested in test equipment, using hand tools, power tools, and test instruments.
Document test results, using cameras, spreadsheets, documents, or other tools.
Inspect or test parts to determine nature or cause of defects or malfunctions.
Fabricate new or modify existing prototype components or fixtures, and assemble or disassemble complex mechanical systems.
Analyze test data for automotive systems, subsystems, or component parts and recommend product or component design improvements based on test data or observations.
Assist engineers to design, develop, test, or manufacture industrial machinery, consumer products, or other equipment, including robotic systems.
Troubleshoot robotic systems, using knowledge of microprocessors, electronics, mechanics, or hydraulics.
Perform preventive or corrective maintenance on robotic systems or components, including repairs on peripheral equipment.
Install, program, or repair programmable controllers, robotic devices, or equipment, ensuring compliance with functional specifications.
Design or conduct experiments based on generated data to meet established process objectives, and communicate with medical professionals to assess health risks.
Develop or implement programs related to the environmental impact of engineering activities and conduct environmental studies for safety regulations.
Collect data for energy conservation analyses, conduct energy audits, and monitor energy-related design or construction issues to identify conservation measures.
Advise clients on sustainable design, energy modeling, or climate control systems to optimize energy efficiency and conservation.
Train operators or engineers on safety laws and equipment use, ensuring compliance with safety regulations in engineering activities.
Analyze, interpret, or create graphical representations of energy data, and develop statistical models or simulations using engineering software.
Review architectural, mechanical, or electrical plans for energy efficiency, and advise on best fuel options for specific sites.
Prepare technical reports of findings or recommendations, based on synthesized analyses of test results or other data.
Design or develop systems for automated control, robotics applications, or mechanical systems to increase efficiency or precision in operations.
Evaluate or recommend improvements in robotic systems, machines, or processes to enhance performance and compliance with specifications.
Document designs, processes, or methodologies for engineering projects, including technical specifications and operational instructions.
Consult with other professionals to determine project parameters, design needs, or suitability of analytical models in various applications.
Conduct research and experiments to analyze materials, safety, or the impact of engineering designs on health and the environment.
Create and manage project plans, including budgeting, scheduling, and resource allocation for engineering or development projects.
Design or develop energy products and systems, focusing on the application of nanotechnology, microelectromechanical systems (MEMS), or renewable energy sources.
Maintain knowledge of current policies, technology, or industry standards to ensure compliance and inform engineering decisions.
Supervise engineering teams or technicians, overseeing their work, providing guidance, and ensuring project compliance and quality standards.
Provide technical direction or support to installation teams during installation, start-up, testing, or performance monitoring.
Perform computer simulation of solar photovoltaic (PV) generation system performance or energy production to optimize efficiency.
Develop design specifications and functional requirements for solar energy systems or components, including plans for monitoring and evaluation activities.
Create electrical diagrams for solar electric systems using computer-aided design (CAD) software.
Design or coordinate design of photovoltaic (PV) systems for residential and commercial buildings, ensuring compliance with project requirements.
Evaluate technical specifications and economic factors relating to product design objectives and recommend improvements based on analysis.
Design and supervise testing or control of processing procedures, ensuring compliance with safety and performance standards.
Analyze product failure data and laboratory test results to determine causes and develop solutions.
Plan and evaluate projects, consulting with engineers and executives as necessary to ensure successful implementation.
Design and oversee testing, installation, and repair of marine apparatus and equipment, ensuring compliance with standards.
Analyze data to determine feasibility of product proposals and conduct environmental or performance tests to develop designs for products.
Meet with clients, contractors, and consultants to discuss needs and evaluate designs for compliance with requirements.
Prepare technical reports, sketches, working drawings, specifications, and budgets for proposed engineering projects.
Investigate equipment failures or difficulties to diagnose operation issues and recommend remedial actions.
Conduct or supervise tests, ensuring that machinery and equipment meet compliance and performance standards.
Plan or conduct experimental or stress tests on prototypes of aerospace systems or equipment, and evaluate their performance accordingly.
Stay abreast of new technology by reading literature, attending conferences, and communicating with colleagues in the engineering field.
Select or install heat and power generation equipment that reduces energy use or pollution.
Create design alternatives for vehicle components to increase fuel efficiency.
Design vehicles for increased recyclability or use of renewable or recycled materials in construction.
Design vehicles that use lighter materials to improve fuel efficiency.
Develop specifications for vehicles powered by alternative fuels or power methods.
Simulate fuel cell and motor system information using software programs.
Research or implement green automotive technologies involving alternative fuels and hybrid cars.
Provide technical consultation related to the development or production of fuel cell systems.
Analyze test data to identify defects or determine calibration requirements.
Conduct non-destructive testing to examine structures or vehicles using various techniques.
Document procedures, such as calibration of equipment and non-destructive testing results.
Examine materials or components for signs of flaws using non-destructive testing tools.
